parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff is massive (84 files, 6600+ insertions) and introduces critical new infrastructure (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) alongside a core module deletion (process.mjs). Without file-level access to verify the actual code paths, two structural risks stand out: (1) the deleted process.mjs module may still be transitively imported, and (2) v0.4.6 — the version that introduced retry/backoff and BigModel error dispatch — has no UAT report despite being the release candidate. These gaps could hide runtime crashes in production API-call paths.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs (67 lines) — potential dangling import at runtime
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was fully removed (shown in diff stat as 67 lines deleted). The commit message 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge' confirms it was intentional. However, check-imports.mjs only changed by 5 lines, and the glm-companion.mjs main entry point was also modified (139 lines). If any runtime code path still imports from process.mjs — especially through dynamic import() or indirect re-exports — the error will only surface at invocation time, not at module parse time for ESM dynamic imports. This is exactly the pattern that passes CI but fails in production when the code path is first exercised.
findings.recommendation: Run a repository-wide grep for 'process\.mjs' and 'from.*process' across all .mjs/.js files. Add an explicit CI check that verifies no import resolves to the deleted path. If dynamic imports are used, add a smoke test that exercises every command path to force module resolution.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 despite retry/backoff and error-dispatch changes
findings.body: test-automation/uat-reports/ contains directories for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 with scenario JSON files and run scripts. There is no v0.4.6 directory. v0.4.6 (commit e0a9ba8) introduced 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff' — the most consequential behavioral change in the release series, directly affecting API call reliability. The new retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) are untested at the UAT level. The user focus on 'v0.4.7 sampling-sweep run 70' compounds this: if v0.4.7 is being evaluated, it builds on untested v0.4.6 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Before shipping, create test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ with scenarios that exercise: (1) retry on transient API errors, (2) backoff progression, (3) BigModel-specific error classification, (4) exhaustion of retry budget. Run these against a live or recorded API endpoint.

findings.title: retry.mjs (203 new lines) — unverified idempotency of retried operations
findings.body: A new 203-line retry module is a significant addition. The glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) consumes it for API calls. If the retried operations are not idempotent — for example, job submission or state mutations that partially succeed before a network timeout — retries could cause duplicate jobs or corrupted state. The tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) and state.mjs (32 lines changed) modifications suggest state mutations are involved. Without seeing the actual retry call sites, the risk is that a retried POST/PUT creates side effects that compound on each attempt.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site that uses retry.mjs. For each, document whether the underlying operation is idempotent. For non-idempotent operations (job creation, state writes), wrap in a deduplication guard or use conditional requests. Add unit tests in retry.test.mjs that verify behavior when the retried function has already partially succeeded.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs modified without clear credential/redaction audit path
findings.body: glm-client.mjs handles API communication and was significantly modified (155 lines changed). Combined with the new error dispatch in bigmodel-errors.mjs, there is a risk that error messages, stack traces, or retry logs could leak API keys or auth tokens. The commit history shows 'docs: redact internal codenames / quotes / hostnames for public release' (560126b) which suggests sensitive data has leaked before. The new error classification layer could stringify or log error responses that contain authorization headers or query parameters.
findings.recommendation: Search glm-client.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, and retry.mjs for any error serialization, logging, or message construction that includes request headers, URLs, or response bodies. Ensure API keys and tokens are stripped before any error is surfaced to the user or written to logs.

next_steps: Grep entire codebase for references to the deleted process.mjs to rule out dangling imports at runtime.
Create and execute v0.4.6 UAT scenarios covering retry, backoff, and BigModel error dispatch before any v0.4.7 evaluation.
Audit retry.mjs call sites in glm-client.mjs for idempotency guarantees on retried operations.
Search all new/modified library modules for credential leakage in error messages and logs.
